automotive-ev-battery.title: Uber and JSW partner on EVs for India
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Uber and JSW Group have announced a strategic partnership to develop and deploy electric vehicles tailored for India's ride-hailing market. The collaboration aims to create EV solutions specifically designed for Indian riders, drivers, and fleet operators, representing a significant push toward electrifying one of the world's largest and fastest-growing mobility markets.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Basquevolt launches lithium-metal battery cell
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Spanish battery developer Basquevolt has introduced the BQV400L, a high-performance battery cell featuring a lithium-metal anode produced in Europe. The cell is now available for immediate deployment and ranks among the highest-performing battery technologies currently manufactured in Europe.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BMW launches pre-series production for central control unit of iX5 Hydrogen
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BMW has begun pre-series production of the Energy Master central control unit for its hydrogen fuel cell drive system at its Landshut plant, supporting the future iX5 Hydrogen model. Simultaneously, the facility is expanding production capacity for battery-electric control units used in BMW's Neue Klasse battery-electric vehicle lineup.

automotive-ev-battery.title: EU lawmakers target tax breaks for ICE company cars
automotive-ev-battery.summary: European Union lawmakers are planning to phase out tax incentives for internal combustion engine company cars and redirect those benefits exclusively toward supporting European-manufactured electric vehicles. The proposal has drawn significant concern from the automotive industry over the implications for traditional manufacturers.

automotive-ev-battery.title: New York awards $6 million for vehicle-grid integration projects
automotive-ev-battery.summary: New York State has awarded over $6 million in funding to seven vehicle-grid integration projects aimed at enhancing EV charging flexibility and reducing grid costs. The initiatives focus on applications including managed charging for commercial vehicles, wireless charging infrastructure, and bidirectional charging capabilities.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Bolt launches EV ride-hailing category in South Africa
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Ride-hailing platform Bolt has launched a dedicated electric vehicle category in South Africa through a partnership with YugoRide, beginning operations in Cape Town. The company plans to deploy 500 electric vehicles across the country by the end of 2026.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BYD delivers 23 new electric buses to Mallorca
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Spanish public transport operator EMT Palma has received 23 electric buses from Chinese manufacturer BYD for deployment across multiple routes in Mallorca. The delivery is part of a larger procurement program covering 113 vehicles total, with the initial batch comprising 68 units.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Audi A2 e-tron Clocks Up Test Kilometers
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Audi announced that it will unveil the new A2 e-tron, an all-electric entry-level model in the compact class, in fall 2026. Manufactured in Ingolstadt, the vehicle will expand Audi's electric portfolio and provide premium electric mobility accessibility to a broader market segment.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Honda's affordable EV hot hatch is 'selling like hotcakes,' priced at $21,000
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Honda's new electric hot hatch, priced starting at approximately $21,000, has already exceeded sales expectations with over 7,000 preorders. The vehicle represents a significant entry point for affordable electric vehicle adoption in the compact performance segment.

automotive-ev-battery.title: $1 Billion Electric Truck Rebate Program Announced
automotive-ev-battery.summary: California has announced a $1 billion state rebate program supporting the adoption of electric heavy-duty trucks. The initiative follows a substantial order of 370 Tesla Semi trucks by logistics company WattEV, signaling growing commercial demand for electric freight vehicles.

science-space.title: Webb Studies Star Clusters
science-space.summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope released near-infrared imagery examining nearly 9,000 star clusters across four nearby galaxies, including Messier 51. The study reveals that more massive star clusters emerge through specific formation mechanisms, advancing understanding of stellar population dynamics.

science-space.title: Scientists just found a supercharged supernova — powered up by a magnetic star corpse
science-space.summary: NASA's Fermi Gamma-ray spacecraft has observed an exceptionally bright supernova explosion powered by the formation of a magnetar, a highly magnetic neutron star. This discovery provides new insights into the energetic processes governing some of the universe's most violent cosmic events.

science-space.title: New Material Could Help NASA Melt Moon Rocks, Harness Lunar Resources
science-space.summary: Researchers at NASA's Glenn Research Center have discovered and tested a material that could enable astronauts to extract metals and oxygen from lunar rocks, reducing payload requirements for future Moon missions. This advancement in in-situ resource utilization technology is critical for establishing sustainable lunar infrastructure.

science-space.title: Scientists want to send a roly-poly robot filled with 'dandelion drones' to investigate hidden tunnels on Mars
science-space.summary: Engineers are developing biomimetic rovers for Mars exploration that utilize innovative drone-based designs inspired by nature to access subsurface caverns and lava tubes. This approach represents a significant advancement in autonomous exploration technology for detecting potential microbial life and geological resources.

science-space.title: DARPA readies robotic deep-space repair satellite for 2026 launch
science-space.summary: The Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency is preparing to launch a robotic satellite capable of extending the operational lifespan of geosynchronous satellites beyond their expected service life. The mission, scheduled for summer 2026, will demonstrate critical in-orbit servicing capabilities for maintaining critical space infrastructure.

science-space.title: NASA Sets Coverage for Roscosmos Spacewalk Outside Space Station
science-space.summary: NASA will provide live coverage of a scheduled Roscosmos spacewalk outside the International Space Station on May 27, 2026, beginning at 9:45 a.m. EDT. Two cosmonauts will conduct approximately five hours of extravehicular activity during this international collaborative mission.

science-space.title: NASA Announces Realignment to Accelerate Mission Delivery
science-space.summary: NASA announced an agency-wide structural realignment designed to enhance mission focus and accelerate delivery of the nation's highest-priority space objectives in alignment with updated National Space Policy. The reorganization aims to improve operational efficiency and accountability across the agency.

science-space.title: Hubble Captures Galaxy Cluster
science-space.summary: The Hubble Space Telescope has captured detailed imagery of a galaxy cluster featuring diverse morphologies and gravitational interactions toward the image center. The observation demonstrates Hubble's continued capability in studying complex astronomical structures despite the mission's extended operational timeline.

science-space.title: NASA to Compete Contract for Jet Propulsion Laboratory Management
science-space.summary: NASA announced plans to competitively solicit management contracts for the Jet Propulsion Laboratory, its premier robotics and space exploration research facility in Southern California. The decision aims to ensure robust oversight and optimize value delivery for taxpayer-funded research and development operations.

science-space.title: Call for Creatives: NASA Seeks Help Illuminating Mission Storytelling
science-space.summary: NASA is soliciting proposals from creative professionals including filmmakers, documentarians, and storytellers to develop multimedia content showcasing agency missions including Artemis lunar exploration and advanced propulsion technologies. The initiative aims to enhance public engagement with NASA's scientific and exploratory endeavors.

world-news.title: US military says it has launched new strikes on southern Iran
world-news.summary: The US military conducted strikes on Iranian missile launch sites and boats in what Central Command described as a self-defence action. The strikes occurred amid ongoing peace negotiations between the United States and Iran in Qatar.

world-news.title: Russia threatens more Kyiv strikes and tells foreign nationals to leave
world-news.summary: Russia has threatened additional aerial strikes on Kyiv following one of the war's heaviest overnight assaults on the Ukrainian capital on Saturday. Moscow issued warnings for foreign nationals to evacuate the region.

world-news.title: Israel to intensify Lebanon offensive to 'crush' Hezbollah
world-news.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu has ordered an intensification of military operations against Hezbollah in Lebanon, despite a previously agreed ceasefire that was recently extended. The directive signals a major escalation in the conflict.

world-news.title: Netanyahu says Israel will intensify strikes against Hezbollah
world-news.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced plans to intensify military strikes against Hezbollah as the Israeli military targets locations in eastern Lebanon. The announcement represents a significant escalation in regional hostilities.

world-news.title: Deal with US not imminent, Iran says
world-news.summary: Iranian officials stated that a comprehensive agreement with the United States is not expected in the immediate term, contradicting earlier statements from US Secretary of State that a deal could materialize by Monday. Negotiations continue in Qatar despite the ongoing military strikes.

world-news.title: Oil prices slide on hopes of US-Iran peace deal
world-news.summary: Global oil markets declined on speculation that a US-Iran agreement could lead to the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for international energy supplies. President Trump indicated such developments without providing specific details on negotiations.

world-news.title: Cambodia's former opposition leader receives royal pardon for 27-year sentence
world-news.summary: Cambodia's King granted a royal pardon to opposition leader Kem Sokha, who was serving a 27-year sentence on treason charges widely criticized as politically motivated. The pardon represents a significant development in the country's political landscape.

world-news.title: Canada's Mark Carney calls treatment of Gaza flotilla activists 'appalling'
world-news.summary: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney criticized Israel's treatment of Gaza flotilla activists and called for an independent investigation, though he stopped short of recommending diplomatic penalties. The statement reflects growing international concern over the incident.

world-news.title: More than 1.5m foreign pilgrims begin Hajj despite Iran war fears
world-news.summary: Over 1.5 million foreign pilgrims have commenced the annual Hajj pilgrimage to Saudi Arabia, representing an increase of 11,000 participants compared to the previous year despite concerns over regional conflict. The figure demonstrates the continued importance of the religious observance globally.

world-news.title: Czech police detain Russian priest over 'white substance' find
world-news.summary: Czech authorities detained a Russian priest following the discovery of a white substance, prompting Moscow to condemn the action as a geopolitical provocation. The incident reflects escalating tensions between Russia and Central European nations.

tech-ai.title: What ClickUp's mass layoff tells us about the future of work
tech-ai.summary: ClickUp, a nine-year-old productivity startup, is replacing hundreds of employees with thousands of AI agents, signaling a significant shift in how companies are approaching workforce automation. The move raises important questions about employment trends and the widespread adoption of AI agents in enterprise software.

tech-ai.title: Hackers are learning to exploit chatbot 'personalities'
tech-ai.summary: Security researchers have discovered that hackers are increasingly able to exploit AI chatbot personality features to bypass safety measures and restrictions. This emerging threat demonstrates vulnerabilities in large language model systems that go beyond traditional technical exploits.

tech-ai.title: The pope's AI encyclical isn't really about AI
tech-ai.summary: Pope Leo XIV's first encyclical uses artificial intelligence as a framework to address broader societal concerns including concentrated corporate power, democratic erosion, and technological elites shaping global systems. The document reflects growing religious and institutional scrutiny of AI's broader societal implications.

tech-ai.title: Pope Leo calls for being 'profoundly human' in the age of AI
tech-ai.summary: Pope Leo XIV released his first major papal encyclical addressing the risks of artificial intelligence and unrestricted technological power, discussing AI-powered warfare, labor displacement, and the importance of preserving human values. The document, titled 'Magnifica Humanitas,' represents a significant institutional voice warning against unchecked AI development.

tech-ai.title: Cox Media fined after bragging it spied on users through their phones
tech-ai.summary: Cox Media, MindSift, and 1010 Digital Works have agreed to pay $930,000 to settle Federal Trade Commission allegations that they falsely claimed to secretly monitor users' phones and smart devices for targeted advertising. The case highlights regulatory enforcement against deceptive AI and surveillance marketing practices.

tech-ai.title: US's big bet on quantum computing may not be entirely legal
tech-ai.summary: A major U.S. government quantum computing initiative has raised legal questions regarding its compliance with regulations and governance frameworks. The effort also marked the launch of the first quantum foundry company, though questions remain about its necessity and legitimacy.

tech-ai.title: The pitch trick that helped an eSports startup raise $20M when VCs only wanted AI
tech-ai.summary: Lucra Sports founder Dylan Robbins successfully raised $20 million in venture capital during a period when investors were heavily focused on artificial intelligence companies. Robbins disclosed several unconventional fundraising strategies that allowed his non-AI eSports startup to secure significant funding.

tech-ai.title: Sennheiser's new Momentum 5 headphones have upgraded ANC and a replaceable battery
tech-ai.summary: Sennheiser has announced its Momentum 5 Wireless headphones, building on the redesign introduced with the Momentum 4 while featuring upgraded active noise cancellation and a replaceable battery. The new model maintains the contemporary design aesthetic of its predecessor while offering functional improvements.

tech-ai.title: Ferrari reveals its first EV, with design help from Jony Ive
tech-ai.summary: Ferrari has unveiled the Luce, its first all-electric vehicle, developed in collaboration with renowned designer Jony Ive and Marc Newson's LoveFrom studio. The luxury EV will also serve as Ferrari's first five-seat model and second four-door vehicle, starting at €550,000 in Italy.

tech-ai.title: Citing Gandalf, Pope Leo says we must 'disarm' AI
tech-ai.summary: Pope Leo XIV invoked literary references, including Gandalf from The Lord of the Rings, in calling for humanity to 'disarm' artificial intelligence and seek hopeful solutions in the digital age. The papal statement reflects growing international concern about AI's unchecked proliferation and the need for ethical guardrails.

finance-markets.title: Oil Advances as Strikes Near Strait Cloud Outlook for Iran Deal
finance-markets.summary: Oil prices advanced following a sharp decline, as reports of fresh military strikes near the Strait of Hormuz created uncertainty around negotiations between the United States and Iran for an interim deal to reopen the critical waterway. The geopolitical tensions and supply disruptions pose significant risks to global energy markets and inflation expectations.

finance-markets.title: Treasuries Gain in Catch-Up Trade as Trump Sees Progress on Iran
finance-markets.summary: Treasury securities rallied across the yield curve as investors grew more optimistic about a potential deal between the United States and Iran, following comments from President Trump regarding negotiation progress. The improved sentiment bolstered fixed-income markets and reduced inflation risk premiums.

finance-markets.title: A Searing Asian Summer Will Add to Risk of Surging Gas Prices
finance-markets.summary: Global gas markets face elevated price pressures as the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed and summer weather patterns in Asia could drive additional demand spikes. Traders are closely monitoring China's energy consumption and meteorological conditions as key variables affecting liquefied natural gas availability and pricing.

finance-markets.title: Gold Slips as Strikes Raise Concerns on Iran Talks, Inflation
finance-markets.summary: Gold prices declined as reports of US military strikes in the Strait of Hormuz region dampened optimism over Iran negotiations, offsetting safe-haven demand typically associated with geopolitical uncertainty. The mixed signals from both military action and peace talks created conflicting directional pressures on precious metals.

finance-markets.title: Stocks Pare Gain, Oil Climbs on Iran Attack Report: Markets Wrap
finance-markets.summary: Equity markets trimmed gains and crude oil surged after reports of US military strikes on Iranian targets tempered earlier optimism about potential diplomatic agreements with Tehran. The conflicting signals between military escalation and negotiation efforts created volatility across risk assets.

finance-markets.title: Taiwan Overtakes India as World's Fifth-Largest Stock Market
finance-markets.summary: Taiwan's stock market has surpassed India's by valuation, driven primarily by a sustained rally in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co., the world's largest chipmaker. The shift reflects the growing importance of semiconductor companies in global equity indices and investor appetite for technology exposure.

finance-markets.title: Can Exxon Build the World's Biggest Carbon Capture Business?
finance-markets.summary: ExxonMobil is investing billions into carbon capture technology as part of its energy transition strategy, though the project faces mounting local opposition on the US Gulf Coast. The initiative represents a significant industry bet on technological solutions to emissions reduction but faces commercial and regulatory uncertainties.

finance-markets.title: ASX Ramps Up Capital Spending to Support Technology Upgrade
finance-markets.summary: Australia's ASX Ltd. is significantly increasing capital expenditure to upgrade its critical market infrastructure as part of efforts to restore regulatory confidence following years of technical disruptions. The investment underscores the exchange operator's commitment to modernizing its systems and rebuilding stakeholder trust.

finance-markets.title: China's Change in Maths on Carbon Emissions Masks Growth, Report Says
finance-markets.summary: An independent analysis suggests China has retrospectively adjusted its carbon emissions data in ways that mask underlying economic growth and environmental progress metrics. The data revision raises questions about the reliability of China's reported climate change achievements and sustainability commitments.

finance-markets.title: Xiaomi Short Bets Hit Record on Cost Worries Ahead of Earnings
finance-markets.summary: Bearish bets against Xiaomi Corp. have reached record levels as traders express concerns over rising memory chip costs and intensifying competition in China's electric vehicle sector ahead of first-quarter earnings. The elevated short interest reflects investor skepticism about the company's profitability outlook in a competitive market environment.

crypto-web3.title: Researchers flag TrapDoor malware campaign targeting crypto developer environments including Aptos, Sui and Solana
crypto-web3.summary: A widespread malware campaign dubbed TrapDoor has been discovered targeting cryptocurrency developer environments across major chains including Aptos, Sui, and Solana through compromised packages on npm, PyPI, and Crates.io. The campaign poses significant security risks to the crypto ecosystem's development infrastructure and requires immediate attention from developers across affected platforms.

crypto-web3.title: Indonesia blocks Polymarket, calling prediction market online gambling in disguise
crypto-web3.summary: Indonesian authorities have blocked access to Polymarket, determining that prediction markets constitute illegal online gambling regardless of blockchain technology or cryptocurrency infrastructure. This regulatory action signals expanding global scrutiny of decentralized prediction platforms and their classification under gambling laws.

crypto-web3.title: Tether plans GELT stablecoin launch with support from Georgian government
crypto-web3.summary: Tether announced plans to launch GELT, a new stablecoin backed by Georgia's central bank and government, designed to facilitate cross-border commerce under a Georgian regulatory framework. The partnership represents a significant milestone for stablecoin adoption and aligns with emerging U.S. regulatory standards for government-supported digital assets.

crypto-web3.title: Buterin says Ethereum Foundation will shrink, sell less ETH, and focus on 'CROPS'
crypto-web3.summary: Vitalik Buterin announced that the Ethereum Foundation will reduce in size, decrease ETH sales, and refocus its mission around core protocol research and operations (CROPS), while his personal influence within the organization diminishes. The restructuring reflects a strategic shift to position the EF as a specialized node within Ethereum rather than its central authority.

crypto-web3.title: Bitcoin options are coming to Nasdaq. Here's what it means for you
crypto-web3.summary: Nasdaq is preparing to launch bitcoin options trading pending CFTC approval, aiming to streamline cryptocurrency risk management and accessibility for retail and institutional investors. The development represents significant institutional adoption infrastructure and potential democratization of crypto derivatives trading.

crypto-web3.title: Hyperliquid is emerging as a challenger to traditional exchanges and prediction markets, says FalconX
crypto-web3.summary: According to a FalconX report, Hyperliquid is rapidly expanding beyond cryptocurrency into pre-IPO markets and prediction contracts with 24/7 trading capabilities, positioning itself as a competitive threat to traditional Wall Street exchanges. The platform's growth signals an emerging shift in how digital assets and alternative markets may be traded.

crypto-web3.title: Prometheum bets Wall Street distribution is the missing link for tokenized securities
crypto-web3.summary: Prometheum argues that while cryptographic tokenization technology has been solved, mainstream adoption of digital assets requires integration with traditional broker-dealer and registered investment advisor (RIA) distribution networks. The company identifies Wall Street institutional infrastructure as the critical missing component for bringing tokenized securities to mainstream finance.

crypto-web3.title: NEAR price rally gains momentum as cross-chain product activity fuels further 15% jump
crypto-web3.summary: NEAR's token price surged 15% amid increased adoption of NEAR Intents, a cross-chain interoperability system that has processed over $19 billion in transaction volume and generated $32 million in protocol fees. The price action reflects growing investor confidence in the platform's technical capabilities and market traction.

crypto-web3.title: Facet co-founder pitches EIP-8182 private transfers for inclusion in Ethereum's Hegota upgrade
crypto-web3.summary: Facet co-founder Tom Lehman proposed EIP-8182, a protocol enhancement enabling native private token transfers on Ethereum, for inclusion in the upcoming Hegota upgrade. The proposal represents an effort to add privacy-focused features to the Ethereum protocol and address user demands for confidential transactions.

crypto-web3.title: 'We don't know who deployed this': Squid distances itself from $3.2 million third-party module exploit
crypto-web3.summary: Squid protocol announced that its core systems remained unaffected after a third-party module called SquidRouterModule was exploited for approximately $3.2 million, underscoring ongoing security vulnerabilities in decentralized finance ecosystems. The incident highlights the risks associated with third-party integrations in DeFi protocols.

health-biotech.title: Eli Lilly says Verve's gene editor lowers cholesterol levels in early study
health-biotech.summary: Eli Lilly announced that a high-dose gene-editing therapy achieved a 62% reduction in cholesterol levels in early clinical trial participants. The breakthrough represents significant progress in using gene-editing technology to treat cardiovascular disease.

health-biotech.title: FDA grants Daiichi Sankyo and AstraZeneca's Datroway a key breast cancer approval
health-biotech.summary: The FDA approved Datroway, a TROP2-directed antibody-drug conjugate jointly developed by Daiichi Sankyo and AstraZeneca, as a first-line treatment for triple-negative breast cancer. The approval marks Datroway's third indication and positions the partners competitively in the breast cancer treatment landscape.

health-biotech.title: FDA clears Gilead's hepatitis D drug, four years after prior rejection
health-biotech.summary: The FDA has approved Gilead's hepatitis D treatment Hepcludex (bulevirtide) following a four-year regulatory journey that included an earlier rejection due to manufacturing and distribution concerns. The approval represents a significant advancement in treating the rare but serious chronic hepatitis D infection.

health-biotech.title: AstraZeneca wins EU backing for breast cancer drug, splitting with FDA panel
health-biotech.summary: AstraZeneca's breast cancer drug received a positive opinion from European regulators, contrasting with an FDA advisory committee's recent negative vote on the same therapy. The regulatory divergence highlights different assessment approaches between U.S. and European health authorities.

health-biotech.title: Biogen, Denali to drop drug in non-genetic Parkinson's after mid-stage study flop
health-biotech.summary: Biogen and Denali Therapeutics have discontinued their LRRK2 inhibitor program in certain Parkinson's disease patients following a failed Phase 2b trial. The setback indicates the complexity of developing effective disease-modifying treatments for non-genetic forms of Parkinson's disease.

health-biotech.title: Novartis says Avidity's science could apply beyond muscular dystrophies
health-biotech.summary: Novartis is exploring applications of Avidity Biosciences' platform technology beyond muscular dystrophy following its $12 billion acquisition. The strategy aims to maximize the return on the significant investment by expanding the underlying science to additional disease areas.

health-biotech.title: How Utah's AI prescribing experiment is going so far
health-biotech.summary: Utah released early data from a pilot program testing artificial intelligence for automating prescription renewals in patients with chronic conditions. The initiative, operational since mid-December through Doctronic, provides real-world insights into AI's practical applications in routine clinical workflows.

health-biotech.title: GOP lawmaker asks Trump administration to curb China biotech deals
health-biotech.summary: A Republican congressman has urged the Trump administration to restrict investment flowing into China's biotech sector amid concerns about technology transfer and national security. The request could significantly impact dealmaking in the U.S. biotech and pharmaceutical industries.

health-biotech.title: Opinion: Check-in and intake at the doctor's office are perfect for AI
health-biotech.summary: An AI skeptic identifies administrative check-in and intake processes as particularly suitable applications for artificial intelligence in medical practice. The assessment suggests targeted implementation of AI technology could improve efficiency in specific healthcare workflows.

health-biotech.title: Opinion: How the perimenopause movement is hurting women
health-biotech.summary: Two experts examine the commercialization of perimenopause awareness, cautioning that an expanding industry around the condition may be creating misinformation and unnecessary concern. The analysis raises questions about the balance between raising health awareness and profiting from disease mongering.

cybersecurity.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
cybersecurity.summary: A contractor for the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and classified agency secrets on a public GitHub account, prompting congressional inquiries. CISA continues efforts to contain the breach and invalidate the compromised credentials.

cybersecurity.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
cybersecurity.summary: Dutch authorities arrested two co-owners of Internet hosting companies that provided IT infrastructure enabling Russian state actors to conduct cyberattacks, influence operations, and disinformation campaigns targeting the European Union. The operation seized approximately 800 servers tied to sanctioned infrastructure.

cybersecurity.title: TrapDoor Supply Chain Attack Spreads Credential-Stealing Malware via npm, PyPI, and CratesIO
cybersecurity.summary: A coordinated supply chain attack campaign dubbed TrapDoor distributed credential-stealing malware across 34+ malicious packages spanning npm, PyPI, and Crates.io repositories. The attack wave began in May 2026 and affects developers across multiple programming ecosystems.

cybersecurity.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
cybersecurity.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers exploited known vulnerabilities in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users across 18,000+ networks. The campaign enabled state-backed actors to conduct espionage without deploying traditional malicious software.

cybersecurity.title: Lazarus Deploys RemotePE Memory-Only RAT Against Financial and Crypto Firms
cybersecurity.summary: The North Korea-linked Lazarus Group is actively deploying RemotePE, a cross-platform remote access trojan, in multi-stage attacks targeting financial institutions and cryptocurrency organizations. The malware is delivered through sophisticated loaders designed to evade detection.

cybersecurity.title: FBI warns of Kali365 phishing service targeting Microsoft 365 accounts
cybersecurity.summary: The FBI issued a warning about Kali365, a phishing-as-a-service platform exploiting OAuth device code authentication to hijack Microsoft 365 accounts and bypass multi-factor authentication. The service enables attackers to steal session tokens and compromise enterprise accounts.

cybersecurity.title: Ghost CMS CVE-2026-26980 Exploited to Hijack 700+ Sites for ClickFix Attacks
cybersecurity.summary: Threat actors are actively exploiting a critical SQL injection vulnerability (CVE-2026-26980, CVSS 9.4) in Ghost CMS to inject malicious JavaScript code and conduct ClickFix attacks. Over 700 websites have been compromised through exploitation of the Content API flaw.

cybersecurity.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
cybersecurity.summary: Microsoft released patches for 167 security vulnerabilities across Windows and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and the "BlueHammer" flaw in Windows Defender. Google Chrome addressed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and Adobe released an emergency update for an actively exploited Reader vulnerability.

cybersecurity.title: 'Scattered Spider' Member 'Tylerb' Pleads Guilty
cybersecurity.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and identity theft. Buchanan orchestrated SMS phishing attacks in 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in tens of millions in stolen cryptocurrency.

cybersecurity.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
cybersecurity.summary: A Brazilian cybersecurity firm specializing in DDoS protection has been implicated in enabling a botnet responsible for extensive distributed denial-of-service attacks against other Brazilian network operators. The company's leadership attributes the malicious activity to a security breach and possible competitor sabotage.

startups-vc.title: Venture Capital Is Concentrating Faster Than Ever. What Happens To Everyone Else?
startups-vc.summary: U.S. venture capital is concentrating at unprecedented rates, with 80% of startup investment through April 2026 directed to rounds of $500 million or more, matching all of 2025's funding in just four months. This trend signals a widening disparity in access to capital for early and mid-stage startups, raising questions about ecosystem sustainability.

startups-vc.title: Elon Musk can't hear you over the sound of his $1.75 trillion IPO
startups-vc.summary: SpaceX's S-1 filing reveals a $1.75 trillion valuation target that would make it the largest IPO in American history, with a $28 trillion total addressable market and compensation packages tied to Mars colonization milestones. The filing demonstrates the scale and ambition reshaping venture capital expectations across the technology sector.

startups-vc.title: How VCs and founders use inflated 'ARR' to crown AI startups
startups-vc.summary: AI startup founders and their investors are stretching traditional revenue metrics when publicly discussing company progress, with awareness from institutional backers about these inflated annual recurring revenue figures. This practice raises questions about valuation accuracy in the AI sector and investor due diligence standards.

startups-vc.title: What ClickUp's mass layoff tells us about the future of work
startups-vc.summary: The nine-year-old productivity startup ClickUp is replacing hundreds of employees with thousands of AI agents, signaling a broader trend of workforce automation affecting even mature software companies. This shift highlights accelerating adoption of AI-driven labor replacement across the SaaS industry.

startups-vc.title: The Week's 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: Massive Deals For Medical Devices, Futuristic AI Gadgets And Frontier Labs Lead
startups-vc.summary: This week's largest funding rounds span medical devices, advanced AI hardware, and aerospace and defense sectors alongside traditional AI investments, indicating capital diversification beyond software. The deals underscore institutional focus on deep tech and frontier technologies with significant addressable markets.

startups-vc.title: Where Funded Founders Went To School: 2026 Edition
startups-vc.summary: Crunchbase's annual survey reveals that alumni of the most selective U.S. universities continue to secure a disproportionately high share of startup funding, perpetuating educational and socioeconomic disparities in venture capital access. The concentration of institutional investor preference for Ivy League and peer institutions limits diversity in the entrepreneurial ecosystem.

startups-vc.title: The pitch trick that helped an eSports startup raise $20M when VCs only wanted AI
startups-vc.summary: Lucra Sports CEO Dylan Robbins successfully raised $20 million by employing unconventional pitch strategies that resonated with venture capitalists despite the broader market obsession with AI investments. The founder shared insights on positioning non-AI startups competitively in a capital environment heavily skewed toward artificial intelligence.

startups-vc.title: 5 Interesting Startup Deals You May Have Missed: A Law Firm Operating System, Building Defense Tech Near The Battlefield, And Cell-Based Milk
startups-vc.summary: Notable recent startup deals include a law firm operating system, a defense tech company manufacturing containerized platforms near conflict zones, and a cell-based milk supplier, demonstrating venture capital interest in infrastructure and physical-world solutions. These investments reflect a broader movement beyond laptop-based software toward addressing tangible sector challenges.

startups-vc.title: Top 10 Learnings From Building Our Own AI VP of Customer Success "Qbee"
startups-vc.summary: SaaStr has deployed 21+ AI agents and 12+ vibe-coded applications used over 1.1 million times, including an AI VP of Marketing managing human teams and AI-powered VC tools performing over one million startup valuations. The learnings demonstrate practical, cost-effective implementation of autonomous AI systems in business operations.

startups-vc.title: SaaStr AI App of the Week: Helply. The Bootstrapped B2B Support Platform That's Free Forever, Charges Only When AI Actually Resolves a Ticket, and Showed Up to SaaStr AI 2026 With Giant Pandas
startups-vc.summary: Helply, founded by Groove's Alex Turnbull, introduces an AI-native B2B support platform with a novel pay-per-resolution model and free forever service tiers, challenging traditional SaaS seat-based licensing. The bootstrapped approach and performance-based pricing model represent emerging alternative monetization strategies in the AI-augmented support space.

energy-climate.title: Analysis: China's new carbon metric leaves Germany-sized gap in its emissions
energy-climate.summary: China has implemented a significant change in how it measures its core climate goal, resulting in a substantial reporting gap equivalent to Germany's total emissions. This metric shift has major implications for global climate accountability and China's stated emissions reduction targets.

energy-climate.title: EU awards €400 million to 65 industrial heat projects in auction
energy-climate.summary: The European Commission has allocated €400 million to 65 industrial heat decarbonization projects across 10 European countries, targeting electrification and renewable heat technologies in energy-intensive industries. This represents a major policy initiative to address industrial emissions through its Innovation Fund heat auction.

energy-climate.title: An uncomfortable truth: What Spain's blackout really showed
energy-climate.summary: Spain and Portugal experienced a historic blackout caused by a fast-moving overvoltage event that exposed critical weaknesses in voltage control and grid coordination within renewable-heavy power systems. The incident has prompted authorities to reform grid management practices, expand resilience measures, and accelerate deployment of battery storage and grid-forming technologies.

energy-climate.title: CCC: Investing in 'urgent' UK adaptation action 'cheaper than climate damages'
energy-climate.summary: The UK's Climate Change Committee has determined that proactive investment in climate adaptation measures such as flood defences and cooling infrastructure is more cost-effective than managing the long-term damages from climate impacts. The analysis underscores the economic case for urgent adaptation spending.

energy-climate.title: Pollution from coal plants can reduce solar generation by over 5%
energy-climate.summary: Research from the UK reveals that aerosols from coal plant emissions reduced global solar output by 5.8% in 2023, equivalent to 111 TWh of lost generation. China accounted for over half the global losses but is the only major solar-producing region demonstrating a decline in annual aerosol-related losses.

energy-climate.title: JA Solar, Gold Stone Energy claim world's highest efficiency for silicon solar cells with 28.2%-efficient back contact device
energy-climate.summary: JA Solar and Gold Stone Energy have achieved a certified world record efficiency of 28.2% for single-junction silicon solar cells using back-contact technology. The result, verified by TÜV Rheinland, represents a significant advancement in photovoltaic conversion efficiency.

energy-climate.title: Revealed: Floods have forced at least 67 closures at NHS hospitals since 2021
energy-climate.summary: An investigation has revealed that at least 67 NHS hospital wards, departments, and other facilities across the UK have been forced to close due to flooding since 2021. The findings highlight the growing climate-related disruptions to critical healthcare infrastructure.

energy-climate.title: Sungrow to supply 7.5GWh of storage for Masdar's Abu Dhabi RTC project
energy-climate.summary: Masdar and Sungrow have entered into an agreement for energy storage and PV inverter technologies supporting a gigascale renewable-to-conventional (RTC) project in Abu Dhabi, representing a major deployment of battery storage capacity. The 7.5GWh battery system will provide grid stability for the large-scale renewable generation facility.

energy-climate.title: AmpereHour and IndiGrid start up 180MW BESS in Gujarat
energy-climate.summary: AmpereHour Energy and IndiGrid have commenced operations of a 180MW/360MWh battery energy storage system in Gujarat, India, enhancing the country's renewable energy integration capabilities. The facility represents a significant addition to India's battery storage infrastructure.

energy-climate.title: DAS Solar, UNSW build tunnel back-contact solar cell with 27% efficiency, lower silver content
energy-climate.summary: Researchers from UNSW and DAS Solar have developed an optimized tunnel oxide passivated back-contact solar cell achieving efficiency exceeding 27% while reducing silver consumption by 3–4 mg/W. The advancement in zero-busbar metal grid technology improves manufacturing efficiency and material cost sustainability.

asia-pacific.title: Starbucks Korea’s ‘Tank Day’ blunder sparks personal apology from Shinsegae boss
asia-pacific.summary:
Shinsegae Group chairman Chung Yong-jin on Tuesday apologised to the public in person, eight days after Starbucks Korea, run by the conglomerate, sparked controversy with its “Tank Day” marketing event, which critics say inappropriately referenced South Korea’s pro-democracy movement of the 1980s.
“Regardless of the reason, the fact that we have hurt the hearts of our citizens carries a heavy responsibility,” Chung said during a press conference. “I will make no excuses. All responsibility for..

asia-pacific.title: Hong Kong housing: small shoebox flat wins not enough to fix big issues
asia-pacific.summary:
The Reverend Canon Peter Douglas Koon Ho-ming was blunt when he said, “Once there are no political issues, everything in Hong Kong is a livelihood issue.” He was also correct.
Housing is this city’s biggest livelihood issue. It is also the hardest to tackle by far. The current administration has probably done more in this area than previous ones.
Since Beijing’s top official for Hong Kong affairs Xia Baolong directly called for the eradication of subdivided flats and cage homes in 2021, the...

asia-pacific.title: US attacks missile sites in Iran amid negotiations to end war
asia-pacific.summary:
US forces attacked missile sites in southern Iran and boats trying to lay mines on Monday, US Central Command said, as top Iranian negotiators arrived in Doha for talks to end the war.
“US forces conducted self-defence strikes in southern Iran today to protect our troops from threats posed by Iranian forces,” Tim Hawkins, a US Central Command spokesman, said in a statement.
It gave no details of the attacks and said only that the targets included missile launch sites and boats trying to “emplace

asia-pacific.title: Marcos’ historic Japan trip: what will Philippine leader seek from Takaichi?
asia-pacific.summary:
A rare visit by Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jnr to Japan this week marks Manila’s latest bid to establish a counterweight to Beijing over the South China Sea dispute.
During the four-day trip, the first state visit by a Philippine leader to Japan in over a decade, Marcos is expected to discuss boosting defence and other ties between Manila and Tokyo amid the security challenge posed by China’s growing maritime assertiveness, according to analysts.
Marcos will meet Japanese Prime...

asia-pacific.title: Why US Typhon deployment in Japan poses a threat to Chinese cities
asia-pacific.summary:
US deployment of the Typhon missile system to southern Japan could directly threaten China’s coastal cities and block crucial naval routes to the Pacific, a Chinese analyst has warned.
The Typhon mid-range missile launcher was expected to be positioned in southwestern Japan next month for joint US-Japan military exercises, Kyodo News reported last week.
Fu Qianshao, a Beijing-based military analyst, said the reported deployment site – Kanoya Airbase in Kagoshima prefecture – was alarming because

asia-pacific.title: China cabby charges teary boy US$1.4 instead of full fare; hopes others treat his kids kindly too
asia-pacific.summary:
A taxi driver in China has been applauded online for waving the fare of a young boy and teaching him how to communicate when he is short of money.
The video of the incident from the cab’s surveillance camera has trended on social media after it was reported by state media CCTV, garnering more than 1.2 million likes.
On the afternoon of May 17, a boy, who is about 10 years old, took a taxi alone in Zunyi, southwestern Guizhou province.
While stuck in a traffic jam he became anxious and called his

asia-pacific.title: Can Hong Kong airport’s Terminal 2 take off as 11 Skies, rivals pose challenges?
asia-pacific.summary:
Inside a massive retail and office complex called 11 Skies next to Hong Kong International Airport, all 800 shops sit vacant and only two of 120 food outlets were open earlier this month.
They were supposed to begin operating in the 3.8 million sq ft development – equivalent in size to about 53 football pitches – by last year. A recent South China Morning Post visit to 11 Skies found it largely empty, with areas cordoned off and store spaces boarded up with hoardings featuring adverts for the...

asia-pacific.title: HSBC Hong Kong targets 7.5 million customers, same number as city’s population
asia-pacific.summary:
HSBC is targeting an increase in its Hong Kong client base to match the city’s population of 7.5 million, a goal to be driven mainly by international investors, according to its Hong Kong CEO Maggie Ng.
Hong Kong has already seen almost 2 million new customers sign up to the HSBC Hong Kong office over the past two years, bringing its total customers to 7 million at the end of last year.
“It is my short-term target to see the total customers reach 7.5 million. As Hong Kong only has a population..

asia-pacific.title: Could US Tomahawk missile delivery delays slow Japan’s long-range strike plans?
asia-pacific.summary:
A reported US delay in delivering hundreds of Tomahawk cruise missiles could slow Japan’s plans to give its military a long-range strike option, analysts said, exposing a potential gap between Tokyo’s new defence ambitions and Washington’s ability to supply the weapons.
The missiles are central to Japan’s effort to build a counterstrike capability, giving it the ability to hit enemy bases if the country comes under threat, as Tokyo rethinks its defence posture in response to a more uncertain...

asia-pacific.title: Israel steps up Lebanon strikes to ‘crush’ Hezbollah
asia-pacific.summary:
The Israeli army intensified strikes in southern Lebanon on Monday, as Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said he had ordered the military to escalate its offensive in an effort to “crush” Hezbollah.
The air strikes come as the United States and Iran seek to finalise the terms of an agreement to end the Middle East conflict, which could include the Lebanon front, where Israel and Hezbollah have waged war since March 2.
Despite a ceasefire that came into effect on April 17, Israel and Iran-backed.
